concepts.title: Master-Sub Account Number Transfer
concepts.summary: Allows admins to move phone numbers between related master and sub-accounts without support tickets. All number types (native, BYOC) are supported across Zoom Phone and Zoom Contact Center products. Numbers must be in an unassigned state before moving, and native numbers require a verified business address at the destination.
key_points: Supports individual, bulk, and CSV import transfer methods
Native numbers require verified business address at destination account
BYOC numbers can move freely without address validation
Numbers must be unassigned before they can be transferred
Destination account must have appropriate licenses (Zoom Phone or Zoom Contact Center)

concepts.title: Number Management
concepts.summary: The centralized interface within the Zoom web portal where admins manage phone numbers, including viewing, assigning, and transferring them between accounts. It supports both Zoom Phone and Zoom Contact Center product scopes.
key_points: Accessible via navigation menu under Number Management > Phone Numbers
Displays ellipsis menus per number for individual actions and checkbox selection for bulk operations
Move-to-account flow uses a guided pop-up wizard with dropdowns for target account, product, subscription, division, and SIP group

concepts.title: Business Address Verification
concepts.summary: A prerequisite for moving native phone numbers between accounts: the destination account must have a relevant business address created in a verified state. BYOC numbers are exempt from this requirement.
key_points: Required only for native numbers, not BYOC
Address must be verified before the transfer can proceed
Acts as a gate in the move workflow: admin must select business address from a dropdown

user_roles.title: Account Owner
user_roles.summary: Has full privileges to transfer phone numbers between master and sub-accounts. The account owner can enable the feature, manage sub-accounts, and perform all move operations.
key_points: Can transfer numbers between any related master and sub-accounts
Must have manage sub-accounts privilege enabled
Responsible for moving numbers from sub-accounts back to master before deleting sub-accounts

user_roles.title: Admin
user_roles.summary: Admins with the privilege to manage sub-accounts can also transfer phone numbers between master and sub-accounts. They need the same license and permission prerequisites as account owners.
key_points: Requires explicit manage sub-accounts privilege
Must have appropriate product licenses (Zoom Phone or Zoom Contact Center)
Can perform individual, bulk, and CSV transfer operations

task_flows.title: Move a Single Native Phone Number
task_flows.summary: Admins transfer a single native number from one account to another through a guided pop-up wizard. The flow requires selecting the destination account, product type, subscription, division, SIP group, and verifying the business address before confirming.
key_points: Access via ellipsis menu on the Phone Numbers list page
Five dropdown fields to fill: account, product, subscription, division, SIP group
Business address selection is an additional required step for native numbers
Two-step wizard: configuration first (Next), then address verification (Move)

task_flows.title: Move a Single BYOC Phone Number
task_flows.summary: Similar to the native number flow but simpler, as no business address verification is needed. Admins fill in the destination account, product, subscription, division, and SIP group, then confirm the move in a single wizard step.
key_points: Same entry point via ellipsis menu on Phone Numbers page
Five dropdown fields but no business address step
Single confirmation (Move button) completes the transfer
Simpler UX reflects fewer constraints compared to native numbers

task_flows.title: Bulk Move of Multiple Phone Numbers
task_flows.summary: Admins can select multiple numbers at once using checkboxes (including a select-all option) and initiate a batch transfer. This flow includes address verification if any native numbers are in the selection.
key_points: Uses checkbox selection with a select-all shortcut in the column header
Dedicated toolbar button: 'Move numbers between master/sub account'
Address verification pop-up appears if native numbers are selected
Supports moving native, BYOC, or mixed number types in one batch

constraints.title: Feature Enablement Required
constraints.summary: The master-sub account number transfer feature is not on by default. Admins must contact Zoom Support to have it enabled on their account before the move UI becomes visible.
key_points: Must contact Zoom Support to enable
UI options hidden until feature is turned on
Both master and sub-accounts need Zoom Global Phone Number SKU

constraints.title: Number State Requirement
constraints.summary: Numbers must be in an unassigned state before they can be moved between accounts. Assigned numbers cannot be transferred.
key_points: Unassigned status is a hard prerequisite
Design implication: need clear indication of which numbers are eligible for transfer

constraints.title: Feature Reconfiguration After Transfer
constraints.summary: If the number had messaging services, SPAM monitoring, CNAM, branded calling, or similar features enabled before the move, those features must be reconfigured in the destination account. This includes creating sub-account-specific 10DLC brands and campaigns.
key_points: Design should warn admins about features that will be lost on transfer
Messaging, SPAM, CNAM, branded calling all require re-setup post-transfer
10DLC campaigns may need to be recreated per sub-account

constraints.title: Sub-Account Deletion Number Loss
constraints.summary: If an admin deletes a sub-account without first moving its numbers back to the master account, the numbers are permanently deleted and released from the Zoom system. The admin has full responsibility to move numbers before deletion.
key_points: Design implication: warn or block sub-account deletion when numbers still exist
No automatic recovery of numbers after sub-account deletion
Numbers released back to Zoom, not recoverable

constraints.title: UI Visibility for Master/Sub Classification
constraints.summary: The move-to-account option should only be visible when the account relationship is a master-sub classification. This implies conditional rendering of UI elements based on account hierarchy.
key_points: Conditional visibility based on account relationship type
Non-hierarchical accounts should not see the move option

ux_patterns.title: Ellipsis Menu Row Actions
ux_patterns.summary: Individual actions on phone number rows are accessed via an ellipsis (three-dot) menu, which opens a contextual action (Move to account). This pattern keeps the list view clean while surfacing per-item actions.
key_points: Ellipsis icon per table row
Clicking opens a context menu with 'Move to account' option
Triggers a pop-up modal/window for the action flow

ux_patterns.title: Checkbox Bulk Selection with Toolbar Action
ux_patterns.summary: Bulk operations use checkbox selection on each row, with a select-all checkbox in the column header. A contextual toolbar button ('Move numbers between master/sub account') appears or becomes active when selections are made.
key_points: Per-row checkboxes plus a select-all checkbox in the column header
Toolbar action button activates on selection
Pop-up window handles additional steps like address verification

ux_patterns.title: Step-by-Step Wizard in Pop-up Modal
ux_patterns.summary: The phone number transfer uses a multi-step wizard pattern within a pop-up window. Step 1 collects configuration (account, product, subscription, etc.), and Step 2 handles address verification. The Next and Move buttons guide the user through sequential steps.
key_points: Multi-step flow in a modal window
Next button advances to confirmation step
Clear primary action (Move) completes the flow
Dropdown-based field selection throughout
